question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user flood the GraphQL endpoint with concurrent alias-lookup queries so that, during a network partition between the GraphQL service and the PostgreSQL database, all HikariCP connections are held open waiting for the partitioned DB socket to time out (up to statementTimeout=10000ms each), exhausting the connection pool and causing a full service denial for all other users?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of evm-address lookup requests such that, when a network partition occurs between the GraphQL pod and the database, each in-flight call to findByEvmAddress() holds a HikariCP connection for the full statement-timeout window, starving legitimate requests of database connections and causing a cascading availability failure?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any retry or circuit-breaker annotation on findByAlias() so that, during a partial network partition, repeated calls from the GraphQL layer never fail fast, instead blocking Spring MVC worker threads until the JDBC socket timeout fires, leading to thread-pool exhaustion and a complete GraphQL service outage?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sequence of evmAddress queries that, during a network partition, cause the native SQL 'select * from entity where evm_address = ?1 and deleted is not true' to hang indefinitely on the database side, resulting in long-lived idle-in-transaction connections that prevent the database from reclaiming locks and block all other entity writes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(Long)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the inherited CrudRepository.findById() path repeatedly during a network partition so that HikariCP connection-acquisition timeouts surface as unhandled DataAccessExceptions, which CustomExceptionResolver returns as null (falling through to a generic internal error), leaking stack-trace or internal error details in the GraphQL error response?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByAlias() uses a native query with 'select *' so that, when the partition resolves and stale connections are reused from the HikariCP pool, the first post-partition response returns data from a dirty or inconsistent database state, causing the GraphQL API to serve incorrect entity information to all users?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user send a burst of findByEvmAddress() requests immediately after a network partition heals so that HikariCP attempts to validate and re-establish connections simultaneously, causing a connection-storm that overwhelms the PostgreSQL server's max_connections limit and denies service to all other mirror-node components sharing the same database?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a @Transactional annotation on EntityRepository to cause, during a network partition, partially-executed native queries to leave open server-side cursors or uncommitted read transactions on the PostgreSQL side, accumulating over time and exhausting database backend slots?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user time their requests to coincide with a known or induced network partition window so that the Traefik circuit breaker (NetworkErrorRatio() > 0.10) trips, causing the entire GraphQL service to be taken offline for all users, effectively weaponizing the partition into a prolonged denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that trigger findByAlias() during a network partition such that the GraphQL error response exposes internal database host/port information (from the JDBC connection URL) in the exception message, allowing the attacker to map internal network topology?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection-level keepalive configuration in the HikariCP setup to cause, during a silent network partition (where TCP connections appear open but are actually dead), findByEvmAddress() calls to block indefinitely without ever receiving a timeout, permanently hanging GraphQL worker threads?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'deleted is not true' predicate in the findByAlias() native query so that, during a network partition that causes partial query execution, the deleted-status filter is not applied, and upon reconnection the GraphQL API temporarily returns entities that should be hidden as deleted?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user send malformed but schema-valid evmAddress byte arrays to findByEvmAddress() during a network partition recovery phase so that the JDBC driver attempts to re-execute the query on a newly acquired connection, causing duplicate query execution and potential double-read side effects on any audit or logging infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityRepository has no @QueryHints for query timeout so that, even with statementTimeout=10000 configured at the datasource level, a network partition causes the JDBC statement to block beyond the intended timeout window if the TCP connection is silently dropped, leading to indefinite thread blocking?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a connection pool exhaustion condition by sending concurrent findByEvmAddress() requests during a network partition, causing subsequent requests to fail with HikariCP connection-acquisition timeout exceptions that are not handled by CustomExceptionResolver, resulting in unmasked internal error messages being returned to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry middleware (attempts: 3, initialInterval: 100ms) at the Traefik layer so that each GraphQL request that hits findByAlias() during a partition is retried 3 times, tripling the number of blocked JDBC connections per user request and accelerating connection pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the inFlightReq limit of 5 per IP at the Traefik layer by rotating through multiple source IPs (e.g., via proxies) to send hundreds of concurrent findByEvmAddress() requests during a partition, bypassing the per-IP rate limit and exhausting the HikariCP connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user cause a split-brain data inconsistency by querying findByAlias() during a network partition that separates the GraphQL service from the primary PostgreSQL node, causing the service to silently fall back to a replica with stale data, returning outdated entity information without any indication to the caller?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a fallback or bulkhead pattern on findByEvmAddress() so that, during a network partition, the entire EntityServiceImpl.getByEvmAddressAndType() call chain blocks, causing the AccountController to hang and preventing any GraphQL query from completing, even those not involving entity lookups?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'select *' in the findByAlias() native query so that, when the partition resolves and a large result set is partially transmitted before the connection drops, the JDBC driver buffers incomplete row data, causing a deserialization error that leaks column names or schema information in the error response?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user send a sustained low-rate stream of findByEvmAddress() requests during a prolonged network partition to keep HikariCP connections in a permanently pending state, preventing the pool from evicting dead connections and recovering, thus extending the effective outage duration beyond the designed recovery window?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the GraphQL API has no request-level timeout (only a DB statementTimeout) so that, during a network partition, a single findByAlias() request holds an HTTP connection open for up to 10 seconds, and by sending many such requests the attacker exhausts the HTTP server's connection limit before the DB timeout fires?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection validation (testOnBorrow) in the HikariCP configuration so that, after a network partition heals, stale connections are handed to findByEvmAddress() callers without validation, causing the first query on each stale connection to fail with a broken-pipe exception that is surfaced as an unhandled error to the GraphQL client?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CustomExceptionResolver does not handle DataAccessResourceFailureException (thrown during network partition) so that the exception propagates as a null resolution, causing Spring GraphQL to emit a generic INTERNAL_ERROR response that may include the full exception chain including JDBC URL, database host, and port in the error extensions?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nativeQuery=true flag on findByEvmAddress() so that, during a network partition recovery, the JDBC driver does not benefit from Hibernate's second-level cache, forcing every post-partition request to hit the database directly and causing a thundering-herd effect that overwhelms the recovering database?
